content.problemStatement:
You're given an array AAA of length NNN. You can perform the following operation on it:


Choose two indices iii and jjj (1≤i<j≤∣A∣1 \leq i \lt j \leq |A|1≤i<j≤∣A∣) such that Ai=AjA_i = A_jAi​=Aj​.
Delete both AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ from AAA, and append ∣Ai−Aj∣|A_i - A_j|∣Ai​−Aj​∣ to AAA.

The length of the array reduces by 111, and all remaining elements are re-indexed to start from 111.
Find the **minimum** possible length of AAA after performing this operation several (possibly, zero) times.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the length of AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer: the minimum possible length of AAA after performing the given operation several times.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are no deletions we can do. Hence, the answer is 444, the size of the initial array.
Test Case 2 : We can choose i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2. Note that A1=A2=1A_1 = A_2 = 1A1​=A2​=1, and then insert their difference 000 back into the array while deleting the 111s. Thus, the final array becomes [0][0][0] of size 111.
